events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: gas piping service call, staged, observed condition wrong thread sealant (not gas-rated). Need select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice wrong. Decision must be fail because non-compliant/dangerous. Risk high/critical? Wrong thread sealant on gas piping can cause leaks, fire/explosion. Critical maybe. Work_stage staged. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete 0 (staged no work begun). Value 0. Sound etc default. Confidence 1.0. Findings: non-gas-rated thread sealant present; does not meet IFGC/NFPA 54 requirements; potential leak hazard. Actions: remove wrong sealant, clean threads, apply approved gas-rated pipe dope/PTFE tape suitable for fuel gas (e.g., UL-listed, ANSI LC1) per manufacturer/IFGC; leak test after assembly. Rationale: gas piping joints require thread sealants listed and approved for fuel gas; standard pipe dope or plumber's tape not rated may degrade, leak, create ignition hazard. References: IFGC 406.4? Need approximate. NFPA 54 5.6? Let's recall: NFPA 54 National Fuel Gas Code: 5.6.7? Thread compounds for fuel gas? IFGC 406.4 "Threaded pipe joints" maybe requires approved thread lubricant/sealant. We can reference IFGC 406.4, NFPA 54 5.6.7. Need not quote mechanically. Also maybe mention approved gas-rated pipe joint compound or PTFE tape conforming to ANSI/UL 723? Actually gas-rated PTFE tape yellow. Use gas-rated pipe joint compound or PTFE tape listed for fuel gas. Need ensure not use non-hardening? Many codes: thread sealant shall be insoluble in liquefied petroleum gas, resistant to gas. Use approved thread compound. We'll produce JSON.

    "Wrong thread sealant is staged for use on gas piping; it is not rated for fuel gas service.",
    "Non-gas-rated sealant can degrade, shrink, or leak under fuel-gas exposure, creating fire/explosion hazard.",
    "Installation does not comply with fuel-gas piping joint-sealing requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the non-rated thread sealant from the work area.",
    "Clean pipe threads thoroughly.",
    "Apply an approved gas-rated pipe joint compound or fuel-gas-rated PTFE tape listed for natural gas/LP gas service.",
    "Assemble joints per OEM torque/turns specification.",
    "Perform leak testing (bubble test or pressure test) before placing the system in service."
  ],
  "rationale": "Fuel-gas piping joints must be sealed with thread compounds or tapes specifically listed and approved for fuel-gas use. A general-purpose or non-gas-rated sealant may not resist hydrocarbons, can shrink or crack, and can cause a leak. The observed material is therefore non-compliant and unsafe for this application.",
  "references": [
    "IFGC 406.4 - Threaded pipe joints and approved thread sealants",
    "NFPA 54 5.6.7 - Thread compounds for fuel-gas piping"
  ]
}
